subject: Chemistry
title: The Emerald Tablet Decoded
description: One-minute skill: The Emerald Tablet Decoded.

hook.text: The Emerald Tablet is alchemy's most sacred text—just 14 cryptic lines that inspired a thousand years of experiments.

buildup.text: Its most famous line: 'That which is above is like that which is below'—the basis of the microcosm-macrocosm idea.

discovery.text: Alchemists interpreted this to mean that earthly metals mirror celestial bodies—and can be perfected like stars.

twist.text: The text was likely written in Arabic around the 6th–8th century, not by ancient Hermes as claimed.

climax.text: Isaac Newton translated it into English. He considered it one of the most important texts ever written.

punchline.text: Fourteen lines, a thousand years of influence. Alchemy's shortest and most powerful text.

quiz.question: What is the Emerald Tablet's most famous teaching?
options: 'That which is above is like that which is below'—microcosm mirrors macrocosm
'Lead shall become gold through fire and patience'
'The philosopher's stone lies hidden in common earth'
